questions.question: Write a C program using OpenMP to find the prime numbers from 1 to n employing parallel for directive. The program should be able to handle large values of n. Also, ensure that the program does not overflow the stack. (10 marks)
questions.hint: Use the #pragma omp parallel for directive and ensure that the work is divided evenly among threads.

questions.question: Explain the differences between #pragma omp parallel and #pragma omp parallel for. How do these directives affect the execution of a program? (15 marks)
questions.hint: Discuss the differences between parallel and parallel for directives, and provide examples of how they are used in practice.

questions.question: Suppose you have a function that checks if a number is prime and you want to parallelize this function to check prime numbers from 1 to n. How would you use OpenMP to achieve this? Provide an example code snippet. (15 marks)
questions.hint: Use a loop that can be parallelized with the #pragma omp parallel for directive and ensure that the work is divided evenly among threads.
